title: Hero — the home-page cover (manifesto paragraph + live capacity)
problem: The Hero is the visitor's first paragraph of contact with the practice. It carries one empathy-first manifesto sentence ('Tu as une affaire plate qui revient chaque semaine...') and a live capacity readout (1 active + 1 in triage = open or paused). Capacity is fetched from /api/capacity at request time — visitor sees the truth, not a marketing claim.
analysis:
Renders DICT[lang].hero copy. One live data source fetched on mount: getCapacityLive (returns the current 1+1 cap state; flips the CTA label between 'Crée ton compte gratuit' and 'Rejoindre la liste d’attente'). Logged-in visitors see 'Démarrer une nouvelle proposition' instead, plus a 'Voir mes sessions' link that resolves to /me or /admin/inbox depending on isAdmin. The body is the h1 (LCP + SEO anchor); the closer is a sibling

styled identically so the two read as one paragraph visually but announce as two distinct blocks to screen readers. The closer span gets italic + sage emphasis. The h1 *is* the LCP element, so its first-paint animation is a colour transition (text-faint → text) rather than an opacity fade — a coloured element counts as contentful and LCP fires on the prerendered first paint while the ink visibly absorbs.

decisions.decision: Capacity is fetched live, not a static claim
decisions.rationale: Saying 'I take 1 active build at a time' is a structural claim — it must be testable. Showing the LIVE state turns it from claim into observation. Capacity cap race fix (AUDIT P1.7) makes this trustworthy.
decisions.recommendation: Don't switch to static copy. The live readout IS the trust statement.

decisions.decision: Empathy-first manifesto, not a self-introduction headline
decisions.rationale: Previous design opened with 'Marc-Antoine, là pour résoudre des problèmes importants pour ma communauté' — a self-introduction. Replaced with the empathy paragraph ('Tu as une affaire plate qui revient chaque semaine...') because the first sentence should name the visitor's situation, not Marc's. Marc gets the signature line. The closer 'Raconte-moi ça.' carries italic + sage emphasis so the eye lands on it before the CTA.
decisions.recommendation: Don't add back the 4-line display headline. The voice has to be 'tu' first, 'je' second.

decisions.decision: Drop the clock-aware greeting and VÉRIFIÉ stamp
decisions.rationale: Previous design carried a clock-aware greeting (‘encore là?’ late-night) + a VÉRIFIÉ stamp echoing the OG card. The redesign strips them: the greeting added a date/timestamp feel Marc cooled on, and the stamp competed with the open-paper layout. The stamp may return later as a watermark; the greeting is gone.
decisions.recommendation: Don't restore the stamp as a corner decoration — if it returns, it lives behind the headline as a watermark or it doesn't return.

decisions.decision: Remove the signature block; split closer into its own paragraph
decisions.rationale:
Design review (2026-05-27, second pass): the .hero__sig (sage hairline + small-caps name) sat between the closer and the CTA as a visual speed bump where the eye should be accelerating toward the button. Hero only needs to do three things — name the situation, name the response, take the click. Separately, the h1 was announcing both sentences (body + closer) as one heading; splitting the closer into a sibling

with shared typography keeps the visual paragraph intact while letting screen readers announce them as two blocks.
decisions.recommendation: Don't add the signature back. If a name credit is wanted, place it BELOW the CTA as a small mono right-aligned line so it doesn't gate the click.

successCriteria:
- Renders body h1 + closer paragraph + CTA + capacity pill
- Capacity readout reflects live D1 state
- CTA label flips for logged-in / waitlist / cold states
- Sessions link appears for logged-in visitors
- Bilingual at full parity (FR + EN body + closer + slot pill)
- One live fetch on mount
- Reduced-motion: ink-absorb skipped, paragraphs presented at rest
implementation:
src/components/Hero.tsx — useEffect fetches getCapacityLive on mount. body + closer come from DICT[lang].hero.body / .closer; closer is rendered in a sibling

with an inner for italic+sage emphasis. Slot-pill copy comes from DICT[lang].hero.slotOpen / .slotFull (no inline FR/EN ternaries — i18n parity contract). CSS in src/styles.css groups .hero__manifesto + .hero__closer-line under a single rule block so they share font-size, line-height, color, and the ink-absorb keyframe.
userGuide: Edit copy via DICT.fr.hero / DICT.en.hero (body, closer, cta, ctaWaitlist, ctaLoggedIn, mySessionsLink, slotOpen, slotFull). The closer span is what gets the italic+sage emphasis — keep it short (one sentence, ends with a period). To flatten the CTA further (e.g., make it text-only): edit .home .hero__cta in styles.css. Slot-pill wording is now in DICT (not hardcoded). Night-theme contrast for the pill uses --feat-intake / --feat-iterative tokens.
knownLimitations: One fetch on mount for capacity. If /api/capacity is down, the CTA defaults to the cold-state label (open), which is the right side of the fence for a hero that should never look broken on cold-start

statusHistory.reason: Component shipped with the offer + live capacity + greeting.
Code-first doc co-located. Live-capacity + clock-aware greeting stable.

Redesign: stripped 4-line display headline, clock-aware greeting, VÉRIFIÉ stamp, shipped counter, bilingual line, hand-drawn signature flourish, HeroShippedProject right-rail. Replaced with one empathy paragraph + closer + sage hairline + small-caps name. Anti-AI-tells pass on the copy (triadic noun list dropped).

_lastReviewPass:
design-review 2026-05-27 (second pass): signature block removed, closer split into sibling

, slot-pill copy moved to DICT, aria-live dropped, night-theme contrast added.

revisions.author: marc+claude
revisions.reason: Hero redesign — empathy paragraph replaces 4-line display, calling-card iteration ported to live SPA.
